name: Aerodynamic Forces on an Airfoil
description: Visualize the fundamental forces acting on an airfoil: **Lift ($L$)** and **Drag ($D$)**. These forces depend on air density $\rho$, velocity $v$, wing area $A$, and the coefficients of lift ($C_L$) and drag ($C_D$). The lift equation is $L = \frac{1}{2} \rho v^2 A C_L$.
renderingCode: class ScienceSimulation { constructor(container, initialInputs) { this.container = container; this.canvas = document.createElement('canvas'); this.canvas.width = 600; this.canvas.height = 400; this.container.appendChild(this.canvas); this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d'); } getSteps(inputs) { const v = inputs.velocity; const rho = inputs.airDensity; const cl = Math.min(0.1 * inputs.angle, 1.5); const cd = 0.05 + 0.01 * Math.pow(inputs.angle, 1.5); const lift = 0.5 * rho * v * v * 10 * cl; const drag = 0.5 * rho * v * v * 10 * cd; return [ { title: 'Aerodynamic Forces', description: `At ${inputs.velocity} m/s, the airfoil generates **${lift.toFixed(1)} N** of lift and **${drag.toFixed(1)} N** of drag.` } ]; } render(inputs, stepIndex) { const ctx = this.ctx; const w = this.canvas.width; const h = this.canvas.height; ctx.clearRect(0, 0, w, h); ctx.fillStyle = '#0f172a'; ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h); const cx = w / 2; const cy = h / 2; const angleRad = (inputs.angle * Math.PI) / 180; ctx.save(); ctx.translate(cx, cy); ctx.rotate(-angleRad); ctx.fillStyle = '#3b82f6'; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.ellipse(0, 0, 100, 15, 0, 0, Math.PI * 2); ctx.fill(); ctx.restore(); const liftMag = Math.min(100, inputs.velocity * 0.5); const dragMag = Math.min(80, inputs.velocity * 0.3); ctx.strokeStyle = '#22c55e'; ctx.lineWidth = 4; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.moveTo(cx, cy); ctx.lineTo(cx, cy - liftMag); ctx.stroke(); ctx.fillStyle = '#22c55e'; ctx.fillText('Lift', cx + 5, cy - liftMag); ctx.strokeStyle = '#ef4444'; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.moveTo(cx, cy); ctx.lineTo(cx - dragMag, cy); ctx.stroke(); ctx.fillStyle = '#ef4444'; ctx.fillText('Drag', cx - dragMag, cy + 20); } }
